title: What's new for AI on GKE: Training, serving, and agents
summary: Google Kubernetes Engine has added innovations for AI training, serving, and agentic workflows. The platform now supports reinforcement learning and complex AI workloads with improved reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness.
writeup: This Google Cloud Tech video surveys recent additions to Google Kubernetes Engine for AI training, serving, and agentic workflows. The platform now supports reinforcement learning and complex AI workloads, with stated gains in reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness. It also touches on model compression as part of the serving and efficiency story. The takeaway is that GKE is positioning Kubernetes as the substrate for the full AI lifecycle, including RL and agent-driven pipelines.
